message:
feat(providers): full tool-call/tool-result extraction for Gemini, Codex output_text support, and Claude Code tool-result serialization

Gemini provider now extracts tool_use and tool_result blocks from both
inline content arrays and top-level toolCalls entries, bringing it to
parity with Claude Code and Codex for round-trip fidelity. Previously,
Gemini tool blocks were silently dropped during read, causing lossy
conversions when sessions contained tool interactions.

Codex provider gains output_text block support alongside the existing
input_text handling, ensuring assistant responses serialized with the
output_text content type (used by newer Codex versions and Gemini
native format) are preserved during reading.

Claude Code writer now serializes tool_result blocks in addition to
tool_use blocks, which was a gap that caused tool results to vanish
on cc->X->cc round trips.

All three providers now skip messages only when they have neither text
content nor tool payloads, instead of skipping on empty text alone.
This prevents tool-only messages (e.g., a pure function_call with no
accompanying text) from being silently dropped.

Additional changes:
- model.rs flatten_content recognizes output_text block type
- model.rs adds unit test for output_text flattening
- Codex serializes user messages carrying tool payloads as response_item
  envelopes instead of event_msg (which cannot represent tool blocks)
- List command header style changed from bold white-on-blue to bold
  black-on-bright-white for better terminal contrast

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -911,7 +911,7 @@ fn cmd_list(
                 "Top {} Most Recently Active Sessions in This Project",
                 sessions.len()
             ))
-            .header_style(Style::parse("bold white on blue").unwrap_or_default())
+            .header_style(Style::parse("bold black on bright_white").unwrap_or_default())
             .border_style(Style::parse("cyan").unwrap_or_default())
             .with_column(Column::new("#").justify(JustifyMethod::Right).width(3))
             .with_column(
